segments.text: Millions of ton of crop waste is set on fire every single year and you all can feel the impacts. But what if that smoke becomes furniture?
This is indoor wood. And they found something I've been waiting for. A way to turn agricultural pollution into something beautiful without cutting a
single tree. I'm talking about rice husk. The leftover waste from harvest.
Get dried, ground into powder, and mixed with natural resins. Heat and pressure transforms into strong boards that work
exactly like wood. Pollution doesn't disappear. It becomes the material which works for you instead of killing you. And if you're concerned about the cost,
there is a buyback program. Old boards come back, get recycled, and become new ones. sustainable and it will help reducing the pollution.
